summary: PR #2231 decompiled and matched a large slice of src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.c with accompanying header updates. Evidence shows many Attack100/item-scope/catch/capture functions replacing placeholder markers, new local structs/includes/prototypes, bot-reported matched-code progress, and review cleanup around empty or unbalanced pragmas plus a reviewer request to avoid inline assembly outside SDK-like code.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation target; replaced many placeholder functions with C implementations, added includes, local structs, prototypes, pragmas, PAD_STACK uses, and attempted inline assembly in a capture-damage function hunk.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftCo_Attack100.c added 888 deleted 85 hunks 30
raw_diff: src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.c diff excerpt
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report with 33 new matches and 4 improvements
review_comments: PsiLupan comments on pragmas and inline assembly

key_files.role: Header surface changed alongside the C decompilation; slice metadata reports 6 additions and 6 deletions, but loaded diff excerpt does not show exact header hunks.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftCo_Attack100.h added 6 deleted 6 hunks 6

what_changed.text: Replaced numerous placeholder markers in ftCo_Attack100.c with C implementations, including item-scope start/rapid/fire/end flow, catch-dash checks, Link/Clink hookshot catch handling, Samus cleanup, and catch wait IASA excerpts.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: /// placeholders replaced for ft_800D76B8, ft_800D7770, fn_800D7830, fn_800D78B0, fn_800D7938
raw_diff: implementations for fn_800D7BDC, fn_800D7CEC, fn_800D7D70, ftCo_ItemScopeRapid_Anim, ftCo_ItemScopeAirRapid_Anim
raw_diff: implementations for ftCo_800D8AE0, ftCo_800D8B9C, fn_800D9228, fn_800D949C, ftCo_800D94D8, fn_800D9C64, ftCo_CatchWait_IASA

what_changed.text: Expanded include/prototype/type scaffolding for the decompiled code, including ftCommon/types.h, itlinkhookshot, it/inlines.h, mplib, baselib headers, ftLk_Init, an ftCo_800952DC declaration, a changed fn_800D949C signature, and local catch attribute structs.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: include additions near top of ftCo_Attack100.c
raw_diff: prototype changes around fn_800D949C and ftCo_800952DC
raw_diff: ftCo_SamusCatchAttrs and ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs typedef additions

what_changed.text: Bot report at one point reported +0.15% matched code, +5844 bytes, 33 new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100, and 4 improvements; the same report also showed 3 broken matches at that point.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: issue_comment by decomp-dev[bot] Report for GALE01

what_changed.text: Review focused on cleanup of pragma usage: an empty pragma range, standalone or unbalanced #pragma pop, missing #pragma pop after a push/dont_inline block, and removal of inline assembly.
evidence_refs: review_comments: discussion_r2900123360
review_comments: discussion_r2900158500
review_comments: discussion_r2900252841
review_comments: discussion_r2900283518

smart_moves.text: The PR grouped many related ftCommon item-scope/catch/capture decompilations in one translation unit, allowing shared local helpers/prototypes and callback relationships to be visible in the same review.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: primary C file changed with 30 hunks
raw_diff: repeated use of callbacks accessory4_cb, take_dmg_cb, accessory2_cb, death1_cb, accessory3_cb

smart_moves.text: The author explicitly noticed excess temp-style names from model output and flagged dislike of them in the PR body, which is useful evidence that temp_rXX cleanup was a known reviewability concern.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR body mentions more temp variable names and not being a fan

smart_moves.text: Reviewer and author quickly identified and acknowledged pragma-balance issues before merge.
evidence_refs: review_comments: author replied 'Ok, just learned what those meant. Yeah, those are empty. RMing'
review_comments: author replied 'good catch'

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not leave empty or unbalanced #pragma push/pop regions in reviewable decomp source; even if a pragma was tried for matching, the final range must be meaningful and balanced.
evidence_refs: review_comments: 'What's up with the empty pragma range that does nothing?'
review_comments: 'Think this pop is by itself? I don't see an associated push'
review_comments: 'There's no `#pragma pop` after this.'
decomp_standards: avoid-pragmas-register-asm

decomp_lessons.lesson: Avoid inline assembly for ordinary decompilation now that objdiff-style function-level matching is available; reviewer guidance in this PR says it is not necessary outside SDK-like code.
evidence_refs: review_comments: 'Let's just not do inline assembly. It was common practice when we needed entire files pre-objdiff, but really isn't necessary now outside of SDK.'
decomp_standards: avoid-pragmas-register-asm

decomp_lessons.lesson: Model-generated output may introduce many temp-style local names; such names should be treated as reviewability debt unless the role is unknown.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author notes the model output more `temp` variable names and dislikes that
decomp_standards: conservative-naming

decomp_lessons.lesson: A matching-progress report can include both many new matches and regressions; intake should record both rather than summarizing only the positive score delta.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists 33 new matches, 4 improvements, and 3 broken matches

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style function names such as fn_800D9228, fn_800D949C, and ftCo_800D94D8 were retained where semantics were not established in the slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: implemented address-style symbols remain named fn_800D9228, fn_800D949C, ftCo_800D94D8
decomp_standards: conservative-naming

naming_conventions.convention: Short role names appear where roles are clear, including fp for Fighter*, gobj for Fighter_GObj*, msid for FtMotionId, and tether_gobj/tether_ip/tether_data in Link hookshot handling.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: multiple functions use Fighter* fp and Fighter_GObj* gobj
raw_diff: fn_800D9228 uses tether_gobj, tether_ip, tether_data

naming_conventions.convention: The PR body flags temp_rXX-style local names as undesirable output from the model; some excerpted code still contains temp_r30/temp_r31/temp_r4 in fn_800D7938.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: temp variable name concern
raw_diff: fn_800D7938 declares temp_r30, temp_r31, temp_r4

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used #pragma push/#pragma pop with #pragma dont_inline on around some functions to influence matching, but review caught empty and unbalanced pragma ranges.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push/#pragma dont_inline on around fn_800D7BDC, fn_800D7CEC, fn_800D7D70, fn_800D8140, fn_800D8268, fn_800D82EC
review_comments: empty pragma range and push/pop balance comments

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK(8) in several decompiled functions, including ftCo_ItemScopeRapid_Anim, ftCo_ItemScopeAirRapid_Anim, fn_800D8140, and fn_800D8E30.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK(8) occurrences in listed functions
decomp_standards: matching-tactics-need-evidence

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Attempted to introduce MWERKS_GEKKO inline assembly for ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys; reviewer explicitly requested not to use inline assembly.
evidence_refs: review_comments: inline assembly hunk for ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys
review_comments: 'Let's just not do inline assembly... not necessary now outside of SDK.'

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduced temporary local overlay/attribute structs to express offsets for item scope and catch attributes, including FighterOverlay, ItemScopeVars, ftCo_SamusCatchAttrs, and ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: FighterOverlay and catch attr typedefs
raw_diff: ItemScopeVars local typedef in ft_800D76B8 and ft_800D7770
decomp_standards: typed-fields-over-pointer-math

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used callback assignment restoration as part of matching control flow, repeatedly setting accessory and damage callbacks after motion-state changes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fp->accessory4_cb = fn_800D80F4/fn_800D86B8
raw_diff: fp->take_dmg_cb = fn_800D7938/fn_800D8378

follow_up_queries: Search final merged diff for remaining #pragma push/pop balance in ftCo_Attack100.c after review comments.
Search whether ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys ended merged as C rather than inline assembly.
Search later PRs touching ftCo_Attack100.c for cleanup of temp_rXX locals introduced around fn_800D7938.
Search later path facts or headers for promotion of ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs, ftCo_SamusCatchAttrs, or ItemScopeVars into owned typed fields.
Search bot or CI records after the final push to confirm whether initially broken matches fn_800DC624, ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys, and ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll were resolved before merge.

notes: The loaded raw diff is truncated, so not all changed functions or header hunks are visible.
The bot report is an intermediate PR comment and shows both new matches and broken matches; the loaded evidence does not prove the final post-review matching/regression state.
Changed-file metadata confirms ftCo_Attack100.h changed, but exact header edits are not present in the loaded excerpt.

Review comments strongly support lessons about balanced pragmas and avoiding inline assembly; they do not prove every pragma or inline assembly issue in the final merged commit was resolved, despite the PR being marked merged.
No local build, objdiff, or final CI logs beyond the bot report are loaded.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Avoid empty or unbalanced pragma ranges in decomp PRs
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2231, review repeatedly flagged empty #pragma ranges and missing or standalone #pragma push/pop. Treat pragma push/pop as a narrow matching exception that must be balanced and meaningful before review.
evidence_refs: review_comments: discussion_r2900123360
review_comments: discussion_r2900158500
review_comments: discussion_r2900252841

accepted_candidate_records.title: Do not use inline assembly for ordinary post-objdiff matching outside SDK-like code
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2231, a reviewer rejected an attempted asm implementation of ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys and stated that inline assembly was common before objdiff but is not really necessary now outside SDK.
evidence_refs: review_comments: discussion_r2900283518

accepted_candidate_records.title: Model-generated temp locals are reviewability debt
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR author noted that the model produced many temp variable names and disliked that output. Keep temp_rXX names only when meaning is not yet proven; clean them when local roles become clear.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR body
raw_diff: fn_800D7938 temp_r30/temp_r31/temp_r4

source_update_candidates.title: Pragma ranges must be balanced and non-empty
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding explicit wording to the pragma/register/asm standard that #pragma push/pop ranges must be balanced, non-empty, and scoped only around code that actually needs the compiler setting.
evidence_refs: review_comments: 'What's up with the empty pragma range that does nothing?'
review_comments: 'Think this pop is by itself? I don't see an associated push'
review_comments: 'There's no `#pragma pop` after this.'
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing standards warn that pragmas are exceptional and should be tightly scoped; this PR provides concrete review evidence for adding explicit balance/non-empty language.

source_update_candidates.title: Inline assembly should remain SDK-only or similarly exceptional after objdiff
source_update_candidates.text: Consider incorporating reviewer wording that inline assembly was a pre-objdiff whole-file tactic and is not normally necessary now outside SDK-like code.
evidence_refs: review_comments: 'Let's just not do inline assembly. It was common practice when we needed entire files pre-objdiff, but really isn't necessary now outside of SDK.'
source_update_candidates.reason: The current avoid-pragmas-register-asm standard already covers this direction; PR #2231 supplies concise historical reviewer wording.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the bot report's 33 new matches as the final validation state because the same loaded report also lists 3 broken matches and no final post-review report is loaded.
Do not infer exact ftCo_Attack100.h signature changes from metadata alone; header hunks are not visible in the loaded slice.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the quoted comments; merge status only confirms the PR merged, not which exact cleanup commits landed.
Do not promote local temporary structs such as ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs or ItemScopeVars as accepted global type facts without separate header/source evidence.
